scenario: A team deploys an Inbound Webhook Connector. They need the URL the partner should call to trigger their process. Where do they get it?
question: Where is the Webhook URL exposed?

options.text: Camunda automatically emails the webhook URL to the partner organisation upon Connector deployment so the partner can begin sending events immediately -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. There is no automated partner notification mechanism in Camunda's webhook Connector. The generated webhook URL is displayed in the Connector configuration in Operate or retrievable through the API. The team is responsible for communicating the URL to the partner through their own channels. The partner then configures their system to POST events to the provided URL when relevant events occur.

The webhook URL is generated randomly for each incoming HTTP request, meaning partners must use a different URL for every event they send -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. The webhook URL is generated once when the Inbound Webhook Connector is deployed and remains stable for the lifetime of that Connector deployment. Partners configure their systems with the stable URL once and continue using the same URL for all subsequent event notifications. The URL changes only when the Connector configuration is redeployed with different parameters or when the process definition version changes.

Webhook URLs generated by the Camunda Inbound Webhook Connector are not exposed externally and partners cannot send HTTP requests to them -- this defeats the entire purpose of an Inbound Webhook Connector. The Inbound Webhook Connector exists specifically to expose an HTTP endpoint that external partners can call. The generated URL is publicly accessible over HTTPS and is designed to receive POST requests from external systems. Security is provided through optional shared secret validation rather than by hiding the URL. The URL is intentionally exposed to external callers.

md:177-184) and/or constructible from the cluster's webhook base URL + the configured Webhook ID. md:170: http(s)://<base URL>/inbound/<webhook ID> (path segment is /inbound/, not /webhook/; <base URL> contains the SaaS region + cluster ID from the cluster's API tab per :172). Self-Managed pattern per :186-190: http(s)://<base URL>/<contextPath>/inbound/<webhook ID> (omit <contextPath> if not configured in Helm).

a.text: Incorrect. After deploying a process containing an HTTP Webhook Connector, the URL becomes accessible. Per http-webhook.md:177-184, Web Modeler exposes a dedicated Webhooks tab on the connector element after the diagram is deployed -- this tab lists the URL for every cluster where the diagram is deployed (SaaS only; Desktop Modeler + Self-Managed users construct the URL manually). Canonical patterns per http-webhook.md:168-190: SaaS: http(s):///inbound/ -- is the connectors-component URL containing the SaaS region ID and cluster ID (visible under the cluster's API tab in client credentials). Self-Managed: http(s):////inbound/ -- omit if not configured in the Helm chart. Note: the URL path segment is /inbound/ (not /webhook/), and the trailing segment is the Webhook ID configured in the connector's properties panel (pre-filled with a random value by default per :21). Team workflow: deploy → grab URL from the Webhooks tab (SaaS) or assemble manually (Desktop/SM) → share with the partner via secure channel → partner POSTs to URL → process instances start. After changes to webhook config, redeploy is required (:175).
